content: When removing a feature built around a shared artifact (e.g. the workflow-design "blueprint"), first map the full blast radius — skills, specs, tests, plugin manifests (description + keywords), AND prose cross-references inside surviving skills — because the artifact name leaks far beyond its own directory. Scrub all *living* docs/code to stay coherent and keep lint+tests green; but for *dated, approved* specs and `.story/` history, preserve them as historical record (a "⚠️ Superseded" banner + neutralised dead links), since they are durable contracts, not live docs.
context: T-031 (2026-06-20): user asked to "get rid of the blueprint and viewer pieces completely." Scope was ambiguous (blueprint = the v0.1 schema threaded through 8 skills, not one piece) so it was clarified before acting → user chose the entire ecosystem. Deleted 8 skills + 5 specs + 2 tool tests (~11k lines); dangling refs surfaced in README, AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, both plugin manifests' descriptions+keywords, and prose in 4 surviving skills (incl. "Mirrors workflow-design-validate" code comments). Two review rounds (cross-file tracer + skill-reviewer) confirmed zero surviving functional dependency.
title: Removing a pervasive shared artifact: scrub living docs, banner the dated ones
